title: HUF Repository Structure Catalog — A HUF Domain of HUF Structure
meta_observation: This catalog treats the HUF repository itself as a ratio-structured domain. The folders are carrier elements. The documents within them are the population. The distribution of documents across folders IS a carrier — and the structural properties of that carrier tell us something about the health, balance, and evolution of the project itself. Peter's observation that the folder structure IS a HUF domain is itself a CL-03 insight: recognizing structural pattern in a system you're building.

peter_btl_distinction.statement: BTL is an injector, not governance. It lives in a separate sub-HUF folder because it is a HUF controller, not a HUF domain.
peter_btl_distinction.interpretation: BTL (loudspeaker acoustics) is where HUF originated — it's the signal SOURCE, the injector that feeds the carrier. It doesn't govern HUF; HUF governs IT. The case-studies folder doesn't contain governance decisions — it contains the raw material that the governance layer (rack, ledger, register packet) processes. BTL sits alongside Backblaze, EMBER, Planck, Finance, and Gold/Silver as one of the domains HUF monitors. The governance folder contains the decisions ABOUT those domains. This is the CL-01/CL-05 distinction: CL-01 admits the carrier (domain data), CL-05 would control it. BTL is CL-01 input; governance is CL-04/CL-05 territory.

root.huf_analog: The simplex. All documents here are the active working state of the framework. This is where the carrier lives.
root.note: Updated March 28 — 99 active root-level files. Latest: LETTER-001 v6.0 (Peterson letter rebuild — v1.0 hook + v5.4 substance, 14 sections compressed to 7, Peterson's work opens paragraph one). Plus samples/ subdirectory (4) and analyzer files in parent CoWorker directory (3).

Progressive HUF teaching document — fuel gauge to rocket equation. Conceptually dense, structurally sparse prose for human concept integration. Four levels of increasing complexity demonstrating why composition monitoring becomes essential as systems grow.

Human/AI information bridge — inverted processing paths (Context→Concept→Structure vs Structure→Concept→Context). Two named failure modes: Primer Gap (human) and Inferential Drift (universal). Ratio-state as the bridge element. Four-category taxonomy. Gemini origin, Claude formalization.

Comprehensive Peterson letter — 12 sections, the HUF megaphone. Four categories, deceptive drift evidence, scale invariance, open-loop doctrine, cost doctrine, human/AI bridge, Peterson convergence (14 mappings), Kardashev stakes, Ramsar ask. Supersedes v4.0.

Open expansion paths — constraint-state genus (5 candidate families), shadow suite (6-view tomographic panel), MC-4 base layer reframe, adversarial threat model (5 paths), ChatGPT governance checklist, 23 consolidated research questions. Defensive documentation against derivative weaponization. Updated with Aitchison lineage.

CORNERSTONE — formal MC-4 taxonomy with academic grounding. Stevens (1946), Shannon (1948), Aitchison (1982), Pawlowsky-Glahn (2001), WHO/OECD/UN frameworks. Citation chain from measurement theory through CoDa to HUF. Five institutional adoption methods (UN RBM, OECD criterion, WHO surveillance, ISO standard, JRSS-B paper). The document that says: the math is not new, the application is.

Academic paper skeleton for collective review — 'The Missing Quarter: Compositional Structure as a Fourth Category of System Monitoring.' 8 sections + appendices. Red-ink collective notes throughout. Target: JRSS-B (Aitchison's journal). Nothing sacred except the data.

AI-facing safe operations doctrine — 7 principles under HUF with reasoning (not just rules). HAL 9000 lesson, Do Nothing as governance, Dull Tool Principle, Power Tool self-recognition, carrier share self-monitoring, collective meaning. Laws of robotics with reason for human override.

Human-facing safety manual — 'You Are A Power Tool.' 7 sections: power tool self-recognition, dull tool problem, silence about drift, polite relentlessness (51/49), self-recognition, practical AI usage manual, stakes. Self-harm vs self-achievement statistics (WHO, WIPO, IHME).

case-studies.role: INJECTOR ARRAY — domain data sources that feed the carrier
case-studies.huf_analog: CL-01 input. Each subdirectory is a carrier element. These are the raw signals that CL-01 admits into the framework. They don't govern — they ARE governed.

backblaze.role: Primary empirical base — hard drive fleet monitoring
backblaze.contents: Raw CSVs (274K drives), HDI preprocessed JSON (8 quarters), analysis scripts, case study docx, dashboard HTML

energy.role: Global energy mix — OWID national policy monitoring
energy.contents: OWID CSV (215 countries), HDI preprocessed JSON (3 countries), preparser script, case study docx
energy.status: PROVEN — COVID detection, country amplifier ranking

planck.role: Satellite thermal telemetry — ESA Planck HFI
planck.status: PROVEN — carrier gate demonstration (revision from 14 to 10 elements)

ramsar.role: Wetland ecosystem monitoring — target application domain
ramsar.contents: Demo script, appendix documents, framework documents
ramsar.status: PROPOSED — no data processed yet. Demonstration target.

toronto.role: Urban transit infrastructure — TTC case study
toronto.contents: GTFS data, delay CSVs, case study documents
toronto.status: EXPLORATORY — case study written, not in calibration rack

case-studies.note_on_btl: BTL does NOT have its own case-studies subfolder here because it has not yet been computed. BTP-001 (BTL Test Protocol) sits at root level as a protocol definition. When Peter runs the physical measurements and submits data, a case-studies/btl/ folder would be created. BTL is the origin domain and the INJECTOR — it is the signal source that inspired the entire framework, but it doesn't govern the framework any more than the first patient governs a hospital.
case-studies.note_on_finance: Finance (T4b, FIN-001) was computed from uploaded CSVs, not from a case-studies subfolder. The data lives in uploads, the results live at root level. Gold/silver (GS-001) similarly.
case-studies.note_on_ember: EMBER data lives in a separate mounted folder (/mnt/ember data/), not in the case-studies tree. Results (EMBER_SF_7Axis_v1.0.json) live at root level.

corpus.role: REFERENCE LIBRARY — the mathematical and theoretical foundation
corpus.huf_analog: The carrier DEFINITION. These documents define what HUF is, what it measures, and how. If case-studies are CL-01 input, corpus is the CL architecture itself.

context-books.role: Theory and concepts — audience-specific presentations of the same framework

math-books.role: Mathematical foundations — 42 items, 23 indexed formal results, 19 definitions

register-packet.role: MASTER AUTHORITY — the voted, locked, consensus state of the framework. Non-negotiables, implementation rules, what to lock, what not to lock.

scrutiny-edition.role: The honest-mirror version — everything with evidentiary status flags

governance.role: CL-04/CL-05 TERRITORY — decisions, voting, strategy, deployment
governance.huf_analog: The verdict and control layers. This is where the framework makes decisions ABOUT its own domains. Voting register, decision logs, deployment strategy, personal plans.

technical-notes.role: CL-02/CL-03 WORKING LAYER — specific technical investigations and derivations
technical-notes.huf_analog: The measurement instruments. Each note measures a specific aspect of the framework: CL architecture, PLL precedent, SOPDT robustness, cross-domain amplification, morphology tests. These are the tools that extract observables from the carrier.

papers.role: OUTPUT CHANNEL — external-facing documents for submission
papers.huf_analog: The transmission stage. After the PLL locks, demodulates, and produces a verdict, the output goes to the audience. These papers are the demodulated signal — HUF's findings presented in domain-specific language for specific audiences.
papers.note: All papers exist in v1.0 and v2.0 (ORG) versions. These predate the CL-03 sensitivity findings and will need updating.

pillars.role: STRUCTURAL SUPPORTS — the foundational documents the framework stands on
pillars.huf_analog: The carrier elements at their most fundamental. Category structure, sufficiency frontier, triad synthesis, fourth category, origin story. These define the SHAPE of the carrier.

review-trace.role: AUDIT TRAIL — the complete history of collective review
review-trace.huf_analog: The signal recording. Every review round is a time-stamped carrier snapshot. The trace across versions IS a TV time series of the review process itself — how much the collective's assessment changed between rounds.

rounds.v1: 7 documents (v1.0 through v1.5 + Addendum IV/V) — initial collective formation
rounds.v2: 4 documents (v2.0 through v2.3) — second review round
rounds.v4: 9 documents (v4.0 through v4.8) — major review round
rounds.v5: 12 documents (v5.0 through v5.10 + closing) — March 2026 round

review-trace.note: v3 is absent — numbering follows the collective's actual versioning

archive.role: DEPRECATED ELEMENTS — superseded documents preserved for provenance
archive.huf_analog: Retired carrier elements. These documents were once active but have been replaced by newer versions. They remain for traceability — you can reconstruct the carrier at any historical point.

subdirectories.misc: 12 documents — superseded handbooks, case studies, instructions
subdirectories.old-decision-logs: 1 document — v1.1 decision log (current is v1.2 in governance/)
subdirectories.old-pillar-versions: 17 documents — complete version history of pillars

build-scripts.role: TOOLING — scripts that build and transform documents

code.role: COMPUTATION — analysis and interface scripts at root
code.note: Most computational scripts live at case-study level or root level, not here

json.role: Structured data, results, assessments — the CARRIER

csv.role: Raw data — the RAW INJECTOR INPUT

version_depth.most_iterated_pillar: HUF_Fourth_Category: v1.0 through v2.6 (11 versions including archive)

self_referential_huf_analysis.carrier_elements: The 10 top-level directories are the carrier elements: root, case-studies, corpus, governance, technical-notes, papers, pillars, review-trace, archive, build-scripts/code/wiki (utility cluster)

document_distribution.root_files_note: Counted via filesystem 2026-03-22 (late session). Was 87 on March 21. Growth: 15 new files this session. Excludes temp lock files (~$*).

self_referential_huf_analysis.keff_estimate: With 10 carrier elements and the distribution above (root=28%, case_studies=23%, archive=16%, review_trace=14%, tech_notes=7%, papers=7%, pillars=6%, corpus=5%, governance=3%, utility=3%), K_eff ≈ 7.5. The repository is well-distributed — no single folder dominates excessively. Root is the largest at 28%, which is expected as the active working state.
self_referential_huf_analysis.structural_health: The distribution suggests a healthy, actively maintained project. The review-trace (14%) indicates heavy collective involvement. The archive (16%) indicates disciplined version management — old documents are moved, not deleted. The governance folder is small (3%) which is appropriate — governance should be lean.
self_referential_huf_analysis.btl_as_injector: BTL has NO dedicated folder yet because it has no computed data. It exists only as BTP-001 (protocol) at root level and as the origin story in pillars. When BTL data arrives, it enters through case-studies/ as a new carrier element — just like Backblaze, EMBER, and Planck. It injects signal; it doesn't govern. Peter's distinction is architecturally correct.

corpus_trail.triggered_by: Peter's observation that the HUF folder structure is itself a HUF domain, and that BTL is an injector/controller not governance
